strategy_name: Evolutionary Trading Algo — 4-Week Paper Run Results v2 (Jump-Diffusion Tier-B Re-Test)
description: Post-v1 iteration. v1 established that GBM synthetic bars produce clean Tier-A (MNQ, NQ) passes but starve the crypto feature pipeline of the jump/regime structure needed to expose edge (all 4 Tier-B bots failed expectancy_R >= 0.30). For v2 we added (a) a jump-diffusion + 2-state regime-switching synthetic bar generator (eta_engine.backtest.replay.BarReplay.synthetic_bars_jump) and (b) parameter overrides per bot. On seed 11, the Tier-B v2c config produced 2/4 passers (eth_perp +0.40R, sol_perp +0.34R) plus 2 marginal (crypto_seed +0.14R, xrp_perp +0.22R). However, a 4-seed robustness sweep (seeds 7/19/31/43) shows eth_perp passes 3/4 and sol_perp passes only 1/4 — evidence that jump-diffusion expectancy is seed-fragile and must be validated on real Bybit bars, not blessed from one seed. No new kills on seeds 7/19/31. Seed 43 tripped eth_perp's 20% DD kill envelope, a useful negative data point for the Firm.
hypothesis: Tier-A (MNQ, NQ) promotion to live-tiny stands. Tier-B is NOT ready for live-tiny: synthetic v2c produced passes on seed 11 but multi-seed tells us the edge is fragile. The correct path is to keep Tier-B at the paper gate, ship Tier-A live-tiny, and re-evaluate Tier-B once real Bybit paper bars are streaming (no longer synthetic). The harness, overrides, jump-diffusion mode, and robustness harness are all production-grade and re-usable when real bars arrive.

robustness_sweep.interpretation: eth_perp is moderately robust (80% pass on jump-diffusion). sol_perp is NOT robust — only 40% pass rate across tested seeds. Neither Tier-B bot survives the robustness bar that Tier-A (MNQ/NQ) meets by construction. Synthetic edge, even with jump-diffusion, does not substitute for real-venue paper trading.

tier_B_unblock_sequence: 3. Re-run harness with --bar-mode paper (new mode) over 4 calendar weeks
4. Re-engage Firm board with APEX_PAPER_RESULTS_v3 using real-venue numbers
promotion_path_proposed_v2.rationale: v2 confirms v1's conclusion: the split-promotion path is the honest answer. v2c on seed 11 shows the pipeline CAN produce Tier-B passes when the bar distribution supports edge, but multi-seed proves it's fragile. Real Bybit paper bars are now the only remaining input that can turn Tier-B's MODIFY into GO.

firm_eval_fields.notes: Scope: Tier-B only. Tier-A (MNQ+NQ) numbers unchanged from v1 (expectancy 0.54R on 308 trades). Multi-seed robustness weakens the Tier-B v2c passes on eth/sol — report this transparently, do NOT claim Tier-B has edge.
